evaluations.title: RAILGUN
evaluations.description: A privacy system for Ethereum using zk-SNARKs to shield ERC-20 tokens and ETH inside a smart contract, enabling private transfers and shielded DeFi interactions.

properties.name: Confidentiality
properties.notes: RAILGUN private (shielded) balances are built through a registry of UTXOs (unspent transaction outputs), similar to Bitcoin. The primary difference is that the RAILGUN UTXO list is encrypted with the sender and receiver’s keys, meaning that no outsider can view the contents of the UTXO.

properties.name: Scalable state
properties.notes: The RAILGUN system contains an efficient internal implementation of a batch-incremental Merkle Tree. Each shield interaction generates a new note which takes the form of a new Root/Leaf on the Merkle Tree. All tokens within the RAILGUN system share the same Merkle Tree. Every interaction that updates the state of the Merkle Tree will generate a new Merkle Root/Leaf that is hashed and secured by the zk-SNARK circuits.

properties.name: Client-side indexing
properties.notes: While generating the most up-to-date merkle tree, each commitment leaf (which represent a transaction) must be synced and decrypted in order to build a RAILGUN wallet’s private balance. This syncing process can take a few minutes. Once the sync is complete, UTXO token balances are aggregated into a user’s balance.

properties.name: Private state model
properties.notes: RAILGUN operates on a (U)TXO (unspent transaction output) model, (U) is in brackets as transaction outputs are completely secure from outside observers. Each UTXO is an encrypted note of a public key that establishes who can spend the underlying asset, amount, token ID, and a randomness field to maintain encryption.

properties.name: On-chain storage
properties.notes: RAILGUN protects this information by utilizing Private Balances, a confidential set of tokens and users where interactions appear to originate from. These Private Balances are known as an privacy set as, to an outside observer; transactions can be sent by anyone who has previously made a transaction.

properties.name: Access to DeFi
properties.value: Access to external, but limited choice of DeFi protocols
properties.notes: First, (1) an unshield is executed against the private balance, moving tokens temporarily into the Relay Adapt contract. Then, (2) a multi-call processes any number of contract calls against the balance. Finally, (3) the result is shielded back into the user’s private balance. This all occurs in a single block and one transaction.

properties.name: Programmability / Generality
properties.notes: Cross Contract calls are the primary method for RAILGUN to access external DeFi protocols and smart contracts. Any number of ordered contract calls can be wired together and executed against a private balance. These cross-contract interactions occur in the generalized RAILGUN Relay Adapt contract, which facilitates a number of serial multi-calls in a single block.

properties.name: Open source
properties.notes: The Railgun smart contracts are open source in Github and verified in Etherscan. There are multiple community-maintained wallets. You can find more information about the wallets in: https://railgun.org/wallets

properties.name: Cryptographic verifiability
properties.notes: Zero-Knowledge proofs are methods for one party, the prover, to mathematically satisfy another party, the verifier, the truth of information without revealing the originating details of the information.

properties.name: Point of enforcement
properties.notes: Private Proofs of Innocence checks begin when tokens enter the RAILGUN privacy system after a shield interaction.

properties.name: Escape hatch
properties.value: Can exit in a time period
properties.notes: With Private Proofs of Innocence, there is an Unshield-Only Standby Period, during which the only available action will be an unshield interaction to return tokens back to the original wallet. This Unshield-Only Standby Period is to give List Providers enough time to update their data such that bad actors are unable to hop addresses quickly to outrun data updates. As always, during this Unshield-Only Standby Period, users retain full control and custody over their tokens and can be unshielded back to the shielding address if needed urgently.

properties.name: Upgradeability
properties.notes: RAILGUN governance is decentralized, any wallet that locks RAIL can vote on and submit proposals to vote. For all governance stages, 1 RAIL locked is equal to 1 vote, snapshots of voting power are taken at each governance stage. Any change to the smart contracts (including the governance, treasury, and voting contracts) requires the passing of an on-chain proposal. The decentralized governance system is the only way in which upgrades to the RAILGUN protocol can be made.

properties.name: Implementation maturity
properties.notes: Railgun is one of the early private transfers protocols in Ethereum. You can check the deployment date of the Railgun Relay in the documentation link.

properties.name: Post-quantum secure
properties.notes: RAILGUN’s zk-SNARK circuits are proved using the Groth16 proof system, a pairing-based zk-SNARK design. Groth16 is based on Elliptic Curves Cryptography (ECC)

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing entity
properties.notes: . Viewing Keys enable the holder to decrypt the encoded interaction information but not send interactions. They merely scan the RAILGUN smart contract events to reveal what has been sent to users and what interactions users have sent.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing control
properties.notes: Users can define the beginning and ending block for which the Viewing Key can observe in a scoped way. This is useful for auditability purposes, for example, a user can define the beginning and end of a tax year for their Viewing Key by block number.

properties.name: Type of compliance
properties.notes: Private Proofs of Innocence works at the balance and transaction level. Meaning, Private Proofs of Innocence proofs are generated after the initial shield transaction and apply to the tokens that are a part of that interaction. They are then carried forward for every following transaction within RAILGUN involving those tokens.

properties.name: Layer of enforcement
properties.notes: Private Proofs of Innocence is a tool that monitors tokens entering the RAILGUN smart contract to verify that they are not from a known list of transactions or actors deemed to be illegitimate. Upon a shield, a ZK proof is automatically created that proves that the tokens are not a part of a pre-set list of interactions and wallets

properties.name: Enforcement entities
properties.notes:
Private Proofs of Innocence List Providers:
Elliptic
ScamSniffer
PureFi
SlowMist
Chainalysis Sanctions Oracle

properties.name: Client-side proving
properties.notes: There is a distinct Groth16 prover for node/browser (snarkjs) and a different prover for mobile (native prover). Both provers generate Groth16 snarks using different assembly processes that are compatible with different platforms.

properties.name: Third-party inspectability
properties.notes:
With this knowledge of ZK proofs and zk-SNARKs, we can see how RAILGUN maintains privacy. Users can prove they can spend tokens without needing to share what those tokens are and how much they have of it. Broadcasters and the privacy set then enhance the data confidentiality for senders and recipients of interactions such that no identifiable information is compromised at any stage in the process.

Broadcasters are also unable to decrypt details about the interactions, nor can they change the details of an interaction as changing any information would lead to an invalid hash and be rejected by the system as an incorrect cryptographic proof.

properties.name: External network dependence
properties.notes: RAILGUN enables encrypted private transactions through Shielding. Once assets are shielded into the RAILGUN contract, there are several options: transfer tokens, use a cross-contract call, unshield tokens. In order to achieve full anonymity, RAILGUN uses a network of Broadcasters to submit transactions. This network is not obligatory, a user can directly interact with the smart contracts

properties.name: Censorship resistance
properties.notes:
Users and wallets are free to use whatever combination of List Providers they please, although some wallet providers will require a default List Provider. The ZK-cryptography generates an independent proof per Private Proofs of Innocence List Provider, which is verified and made available for further usage.

Users can use pools that do not require Private Proof of Innocence even though it is not the standard (default).

properties.name: Number of secrets
properties.notes: It requires an Ethereum wallet for public interactions and a private encryption key for Railgun interactions.

properties.name: Plausible deniability
properties.notes: The shield action sends ERC-20 tokens into the RAILGUN contract

properties.name: Asset privacy
properties.notes: Everybody can see that ERC-20 tokens are being sent to the Railgun contracts. In a private transfer, it is not possible to identify which asset is being transfered

properties.name: Anonymity
properties.notes: The output of RAILGUN circuits are UTXOs and Nullifiers, which is a hash generated from private keys that cannot be linked to a UTXO by anyone who is not a party to the transaction. The party holding the Spending Key is the only actor who can link which Nullifier as belonging to a corresponding UTXO.

evaluations.title: Tornado Cash
evaluations.description: A non-custodial, Ethereum-based mixer that uses zk-SNARKs to break the on-chain link between deposit and withdrawal addresses for fixed-denomination ETH and ERC-20 deposits.

evaluations.title: Privacy Pools
evaluations.description: A privacy protocol that extends Tornado Cash with association set providers (ASPs), allowing users to prove membership in compliant subsets without revealing their identity.

evaluations.title: Intmax
evaluations.description: A stateless zkRollup optimised for private payments, pushing most data off-chain so that on-chain costs are minimal while preserving user-side privacy through client-side proving.
